title: Fallback program path resolves outside the current directory
reasoning: When no module URL is supplied, runtimeModuleUrl falls back to pathToFileURL(realpathSync(process.argv[1] ?? process.cwd())). If process.argv[1] is absent and process.cwd() is a directory, pathToFileURL returns a directory path without a trailing slash, so programPathFromModuleUrl resolves ./program.md as a sibling of the cwd rather than inside it. The package-local path is covered for the normal import.meta.url path, but the cwd fallback path is not. This can produce a wrong default Config.program in embedded or unusual runtimes where process.argv[1] is unset.
recommendation: When falling back to cwd, convert it to a directory URL with a trailing slash, or avoid the cwd fallback for resolving package-local runtime data. Keep file module paths and directory fallbacks handled separately.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing program.md tests cover import.meta.url and URL-decoding behavior, but they do not simulate process.argv[1] being absent or verify the cwd fallback branch.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test for programPath/runtime fallback behavior with an argv-less runtime path, or expose a small helper that accepts an undefined module URL plus cwd and asserts the resulting program path is inside that cwd/package directory as intended.
minimumFixScope: Adjust runtimeModuleUrl/program path fallback handling and add one targeted test for the process.argv[1] absent case.
